content.summaryText: Neonatal Danger Signs in Newborn Care Essentials focuses on early neonatal assessment and stabilization. Primary objective: Identify respiratory distress.

content.questionText: In Neonatal Danger Signs, which action best supports 'Identify respiratory distress'?
content.explanation: Correct answers prioritize patient safety, reassessment, and timely escalation.

options.text: Check breathing, temperature, feeding ability, and jaundice status
Delaying referral when danger signs persist
Delay reassessment until symptoms are severe
Use treatment decisions without baseline documentation

content.summaryText: Immediate priority: check breathing, temperature, feeding ability, and jaundice status. Practical emphasis: Assess feeding intolerance.

content.questionText: Escalation is required when poor feeding, lethargy, cyanosis, fever/hypothermia, or apnea.
content.explanation: Early escalation reduces preventable deterioration and delays in definitive care.

content.sentence:
Start with <1> to establish baseline status.
Use <2> to deliver standardized intervention.
Document <3> to support continuity and quality review.
